steps.title: Check the first equation
steps.content:
Equation I is a constant multiple of the given equation.
$$
6x+7.5y=1.5(4x+5y)
$$
$$
15=1.5(10)
$$
So it represents the same line and gives infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Check the second equation
steps.content:
Equation II has a different slope from the given equation. Solving it for $y$ gives
$$
6x-7.5y=15
$$
$$
y=\frac{4}{5}x-2
$$
The given line has slope $$-\frac{4}{5}$$, so the two lines intersect once.

steps.title: Choose the valid statements
steps.content:
Both Roman numeral equations create a system with at least one solution.
The answer is C.
